topics.title: Google loses EU Android appeal
topics.summary: Europe's top court dismissed Google's appeal of a €4.1 billion Android antitrust fine, backing regulators who said the company limited competition and consumer choice.
topics.discussionPrompt: Android is everywhere, but regulators say platform power can get too concentrated. Do you think big tech ecosystems make life easier, or box people in?

topics.title: NASA satellite rescue delayed
topics.summary: A mission to rescue NASA's aging Swift Observatory was postponed after weather and technical issues stopped the Pegasus rocket launch over the Pacific.
topics.discussionPrompt: A robotic rescue mission for an old space telescope sounds wonderfully sci-fi. Should space agencies save aging spacecraft, or spend that money on new missions?

topics.title: Wall Street ends mixed
topics.summary: Most U.S. stocks rose Thursday and the Dow hit another record, but weakness in chip and AI-related shares kept the broader indexes mixed.
topics.discussionPrompt: AI stocks have been market stars lately, but they can wobble too. Would you trust a market rally led by one hot sector?

topics.title: World Cup fans brave heat
topics.summary: Fans kept up the World Cup atmosphere in Philadelphia despite extreme heat, adding weather and endurance to the drama around the tournament.
topics.discussionPrompt: Big sports events are supposed to feel festive. How much should heat and climate planning shape where and when major tournaments happen?

topics.title: Princess Kate visits Wimbledon
topics.summary: Princess Kate watched British players at Wimbledon, including Arthur Fery's win, as the tournament continued with only a few home singles hopes left.
topics.discussionPrompt: Wimbledon has this mix of sport, tradition and celebrity spotting. Does that extra pageantry make tennis more fun, or distract from the matches?
